skill.name: web-search
skill.description: Search the web, extract page content, and request direct answers using Tavily and Exa apps through the inference.sh belt CLI.
skill.summary: Use Tavily and Exa search and extraction tools from the belt CLI.
skill.author: halt-catch-fire

security_audit.summary: Most static external-command hits are false positives caused by markdown code spans and fenced examples. The skill still carries medium contextual risk because it grants Bash access to the belt CLI and sends search, extraction, and LLM requests to external services.

remediation.issue: Broad belt CLI access can send user queries, URLs, and extracted content to third-party services.
remediation.suggestion: Document the data-sharing boundary clearly and warn users not to submit secrets, private URLs, or regulated data.

remediation.issue: Remote skill installation examples are not pinned to immutable versions.
remediation.suggestion: Pin install commands to trusted versions or commits, and advise users to review remote skills before installation.

remediation.issue: The markdown includes a remotely hosted image and mutable external installation documentation.
remediation.suggestion: Prefer marketplace-hosted assets and stable documentation links when possible.

static_findings.snippet: > **Install the belt CLI skill:** `npx skills add belt-sh/cli`
| Search Assistant | `tavily/search-assistant` | AI-powered search with answers |
| Search | `exa/search` | Smart web search with AI |
| Extract | `exa/extract` | Extract and analyze web content |
Search the web and extract content via [inference.sh](https://inference.sh) CLI.
- [Adding Tools to Agents](https://inference.sh/docs/agents/adding-tools) - Equip agents with search

semantic_findings.title: Broad CLI Tool Permission
semantic_findings.description: The skill allows Bash(belt *) and examples run remote search, extraction, and LLM apps. Queries, URLs, and extracted content can leave the local environment.
semantic_findings.confidence_reasoning: The frontmatter grants Bash access to any belt command, and the documented workflows invoke remote provider apps. This is expected functionality but creates a real data-sharing boundary.

semantic_findings.title: Unpinned External Skill Installation Guidance
semantic_findings.description: The documentation suggests npx skills add commands for the belt CLI and related skills. Users who copy them may install remote code without pinned versions.
semantic_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in the markdown and reference remote skill packages. They require user action, so the risk is limited but real.

content.user_title: Research Web Sources with Tavily and Exa
content.value_statement: Current web research often needs several search and extraction tools. This skill provides Tavily and Exa commands through one belt CLI workflow.
actual_capabilities: Runs Tavily search assistant requests through the belt CLI.
Extracts readable content from one or more URLs with Tavily.
Runs Exa search queries for relevant web results.
Requests direct factual answers from Exa.
Combines search or extraction output with LLM summarization workflows.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh belt CLI and login before use.
Search and extraction results depend on Tavily, Exa, and source website availability.
External services may receive submitted queries, URLs, and extracted content.
The skill provides command examples, not a local search engine.

use_cases.title: Gather Source Material
use_cases.description: Search recent web pages and collect relevant links before writing a report or brief.

use_cases.title: Prepare RAG Inputs
use_cases.description: Extract readable page content from URLs for retrieval pipelines or summarization tasks.

use_cases.title: Check Claims Quickly
use_cases.description: Ask direct factual questions and compare answers with source links from search results.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Web Search
prompt_templates.scenario: Find sources for a simple topic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web for recent information about this topic: [topic]. Return the strongest sources and a short summary.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Article Content
prompt_templates.scenario: Convert web pages into readable text.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the main content from these URLs: [URLs]. Remove navigation, ads, and unrelated page text.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Search Providers
prompt_templates.scenario: Use both Tavily and Exa for stronger coverage.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for [topic] with Tavily and Exa. Compare the results, note disagreements, and list the most useful sources.

prompt_templates.title: Research Then Summarize
prompt_templates.scenario: Build a research workflow with search, extraction, and LLM analysis.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for [research question], extract the best sources, then summarize key findings, evidence quality, and open questions.

output_examples.input: Search for best practices for building AI agents.
output_examples.output: A concise answer with source links, relevant context, and images when the provider returns them.

output_examples.input: Extract content from two article URLs.
output_examples.output: Clean article text, page metadata, and extracted images suitable for summarization or review.

output_examples.input: Answer a factual question about a city population.
output_examples.output: A direct answer with supporting context from web sources, subject to provider coverage.

best_practices: Review source links before relying on generated answers.
Avoid sending secrets, private URLs, or regulated data to external search services.
Use extraction before summarization when exact source text matters.
anti_patterns: Do not treat search results as verified facts without checking sources.
Do not submit internal documents or confidential URLs to remote providers.
Do not assume every website allows reliable extraction.

faq.question: Does this skill search the live web?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses Tavily and Exa apps through the inference.sh belt CLI.

faq.question: Do I need an external account?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill requires the belt CLI and an inference.sh login.

faq.question: Can it extract full article text?
faq.answer: It can request page extraction, but results depend on the website and provider support.

faq.question: Is it compatible with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Can it support RAG workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It can collect search results and extracted text for retrieval or summarization workflows.

faq.question: What data may leave my machine?
faq.answer: Queries, URLs, extracted content, and prompts may be sent to inference.sh provider apps.
